old_claim.note: Hospital Information Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from endoscopy_video_systems to hospital_information_systems; video endoscope architecture depends on optics and image sensors, not hospital IT.

source_shape.source_locator: Review history section on fiberoptic endoscopy and the first video endoscope.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review explains video endoscopy through miniaturized CCDs and prior flexible endoscopes, without making hospital information systems part of the device.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This refutes HIS as a direct prerequisite because the relevant device mechanism is electronic image capture in an endoscope, not clinical records or hospital workflow software.

ontology_before: Video endoscopy depended on hospital information systems, mixing device architecture with later clinical workflow infrastructure.
ontology_after: Video endoscopy is modeled through fiberoptic predecessor context and electronic image-sensor capture.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hospital information systems no longer appear as a direct video-endoscopy prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node were re-scoped to endoscopy reporting software rather than video endoscopy systems.
A source showed the video endoscope device itself requires hospital information-system infrastructure.
Removing HIS also removed the electronic image-sensor foundation.
short_reason: Hospital information systems are not a video-endoscope component.
